meaning: adj.不平等的,不均等的,赞成不平等(或不均等)的
examples: This striving is necessarily, and worryingly, inegalitarian.
Living standards had to be raised in Latin America, then as now the world's most inegalitarian region.
This kind of system is dramatically inegalitarian, as the worst schools tend to be in the poorest neighborhoods.
Even in these economically challenging times, Biltmore retains its power to delight, enticing us back to an aesthetically alluring past whose inegalitarian edges have been sanded smooth by time.
